meta_decks.name: Izzet Lessons

key_threats.reason: Spell-copying engine that snowballs card advantage and doubles burn output
Primary burn finisher; combo kill enabler when copied or chained
Grants resilience and reach, making the combo harder to disrupt
Value creature that generates resources and threatens the board if unanswered

meta_decks.description: Izzet Lessons leverages spell-copying and card advantage engines to assemble a critical mass of burn and tempo, threatening a combo kill around turn 6. Artist's Talent and Accumulate Wisdom fuel the engine while Combustion Technique provides the finishing burst damage. Meta share: 11.9%.

meta_decks.name: Mono-Green Landfall

key_threats.reason: Turns landfall into huge burst turns
Creates sudden combat lethals
Early pressure into oversized board

meta_decks.description: Ground-centric landfall deck with explosive combat steps

meta_decks.name: Izzet Prowess

key_threats.reason: Grows with every instant/sorcery — becomes huge fast
Gives all creatures trample + pump on spells
Drains life on spells, hard to race
Creates flying tokens on spells — go-wide threat

meta_decks.description: Creature-based prowess deck. Creatures grow huge with each spell. Remove Firebending Student and Balmor before they snowball.

meta_decks.name: Dimir Excruciator

key_threats.reason: Ends games quickly once it resolves
Punishes overextension
Turns stalled games into losses

meta_decks.description: Disruptive combo-control deck with sweepers and a fast closer

meta_decks.name: Dimir Midrange

key_threats.reason: Runs away with cards and tempo
Snowballs small flyers into advantage
Disrupts your curve while pressuring life

meta_decks.description: Tempo-midrange deck built on flyers, discard, and Kaito

meta_decks.name: Izzet Spellementals

key_threats.reason: Premier payoff elemental; snowballs quickly when cheap spells chain together each turn.
Scales with spell volume; threatens lethal damage if left unchecked for a turn cycle.
Grinds incremental value off every spell cast; fuels late-game card advantage engine.
Recurring noncreature value source; difficult to interact with via creature removal.

meta_decks.description: Izzet Spellementals chains cheap cantrips and burn to trigger elemental payoffs, building an overwhelming board while maintaining card flow. Dangerous because each cantrip doubles as both selection and a creature trigger, making removal exchanges inefficient. Meta share: 5.0%.

meta_decks.name: Azorius Tempo

key_threats.reason: Constrains your spell windows
Punishes key curve turns
Dominates tempo and skies

meta_decks.description: Tempo deck that wins by constraining mana and sequencing

meta_decks.name: Mono-Red Aggro

key_threats.reason: Fast pressure that punishes stumbles
Top-end threat that closes races
Turns off your lifegain plan

meta_decks.description: Fast burn deck that demands early blocks and lifegain. Monstrous Rage banned, Heartfire Hero nerfed to 0/1.

meta_decks.name: Jeskai Control

key_threats.reason: Sweeper that punishes overcommitment
Cleans up your cheap board
Takes over once they stabilize

meta_decks.description: Removal-heavy control deck with wraths and late card advantage

meta_decks.name: Temur Harmonizer Combo

key_threats.reason: Core combo engine; converts cheap spells into lethal damage if untouched
Enchantment-based alternate win condition; harder to remove than creatures
Finds combo pieces and accelerates mana; enables consistent turn-6 kills
Value creature that fixes mana and replaces itself; fuels combo setup

meta_decks.description: Temur Harmonizer Combo builds toward a turn-6 kill by chaining cheap spells through Mightform Harmonizer to generate explosive damage or overwhelming board presence. Consult the Star Charts and Stock Up provide the card velocity to assemble the combo consistently. Meta share: 2.6%.

meta_decks.name: Bant Airbending Combo

key_threats.reason: Enables casting adventures from exile, turning Bramble Familiar into an infinite loop engine.
Bounce/flicker effect closes the combo loop; without it, no infinite iteration.
Mana production plus Fetch Quest tutor; the card being looped for the kill.
Turn-1 ramp enables turn-5 kills; removing it buys a critical turn.

meta_decks.description: Bant Airbending exploits Aang's bounce/flicker ability to loop Bramble Familiar and Doc Aurlock's adventure-from-exile engine, generating infinite mana and tutors to assemble a deterministic kill. Dangerous because it can threaten the combo through interaction while ramping ahead on mana. Meta share: 2.5%.

meta_decks.name: Boros Dragons

key_threats.reason: Planeswalker that generates dragon tokens and threatens an ultimate that ends the game.
Creates tokens on attack, building overwhelming board presence if not immediately removed.
Board wipe or massive damage spell that punishes wide boards and stabilization attempts.
Large evasive body with built-in removal, dominates combat and picks off creatures.

meta_decks.description: Boros Dragons leverages efficient dragon creatures and burn spells to close games quickly by turn 6. The deck combines evasive flying threats with powerful ETB/attack triggers that snowball if left unchecked. Meta share: 2.5%.

meta_decks.name: Simic Ouroboroid

key_threats.reason: Combo finisher that ends the game immediately if it resolves untouched
Key enabler that copies or generates pieces feeding the Ouroboroid combo
Digs for combo pieces and provides card selection to ensure consistent turn-5 kills
Turn-1 mana dork accelerates the entire combo by a full turn

meta_decks.description: Simic ramp-combo deck that uses mana acceleration and token generators to assemble an Ouroboroid kill by turn 5. Extremely linear but resilient to single-point removal thanks to redundant enablers. Meta share: 2.5%.

meta_decks.name: 4c Ouroboroid

key_threats.reason: Core combo engine; draws into the loop and enables deterministic kill sequences.
Creates the self-replicating loop that generates lethal board state or damage.
Enchantment-based recursion engine; harder to remove and powers repeated triggers.
Copies any combo piece for redundancy; removes single-point-of-failure weakness.

meta_decks.description: A 4-color creature combo deck that uses mana acceleration into copy effects and recursive triggers to assemble a lethal loop, typically winning turn 5 through Gene Pollinator chains amplified by Mockingbird and Nature's Rhythm. Extremely fragile to early interaction but nearly unstoppable if it resolves its engine. Meta share: 1.8%.

meta_decks.name: Rakdos Monument

key_threats.reason: Grows every creature on entry; activated ability grants trample/lifelink for lethal swings
Generates card advantage and +1/+1 counters with trample on each attack
Keeps recurring from graveyard; nearly impossible to permanently remove
Auto-equips on creature entry; turns every body into an immediate threat

meta_decks.description: Rakdos Monument builds a board of increasingly large creatures via Monument to Endurance counters, then uses its activated ability to grant trample and lifelink for devastating alpha strikes. Flamewake Phoenix and Inti provide relentless recursion and card advantage that grind opponents out. Meta share: 1.8%.

meta_decks.name: Azorius Control

key_threats.reason: Shuts down instant-speed interaction; warps entire game around its presence
Recurring card advantage engine; returns as enchantment after removal, extremely resilient
Taps down threats and provides inevitability as a win condition
Flash disruption exiles key spells; taxes future casts from exile

meta_decks.description: Azorius Control leverages High Noon to lock opponents out of instant-speed interaction, then grinds to victory with resilient threats and card advantage engines. Dangerous because it neutralizes reactive strategies while generating inevitability through Enduring Innocence and evasive finishers. Meta share: 1.4%.

meta_decks.name: Sultai Ouroboroid

key_threats.reason: Central combo piece; triggers exponential self-copying chain that wins the game.
Key enabler that starts or extends the copy chain alongside Ouroboroid.
Redundant copy effect that can substitute for or accelerate the combo.
Accelerates combo by one turn; removing it delays the kill to turn 6.

meta_decks.description: Sultai Ouroboroid builds an exponential copy chain, using mana acceleration and copy enablers to flood the board with Ouroboroid tokens as early as turn 5. The deck is dangerous because once the chain starts, a single unanswered trigger cascades into a lethal board state. Meta share: 1.3%.

meta_decks.name: Izzet Elementals

key_threats.reason: Doubles all elemental triggers; turns Ashling into an unstoppable damage engine
Core damage payoff; every elemental ETB becomes direct damage to any target
Generates elemental tokens that fuel Ashling triggers and build board pressure
Sustained card advantage and value engine that keeps elemental threats flowing

meta_decks.description: Izzet Elementals builds a synergy engine around elemental tribal triggers, using Ashling as a damage multiplier and Roaming Throne to double those triggers, converting each new elemental into a burn spell that closes games quickly. Meta share: 1.3%.

meta_decks.name: Sultai Reanimator

key_threats.reason: Returns ALL creatures from graveyard to battlefield; game-ending if it resolves.
Mills and recurs threats on ETB; enables the entire graveyard engine.
Evasive resilient threat that generates value; strong reanimation target.
Early value creature that filters draws and fills the graveyard consistently.

meta_decks.description: Sultai Reanimator fills the graveyard with high-value creatures using discard and mill effects, then cheats them all into play with Bringer of the Last Gift for an overwhelming board state. The deck is dangerous because it can recover from removal-heavy games and win in a single explosive turn. Meta share: 1.1%.

meta_decks.name: Gruul Landfall

key_threats.reason: Enchantment-based landfall engine that snowballs; harder to interact with than creatures.
Powerful midrange beater that closes games fast with landfall triggers active.
Landfall payoff creature that grows beyond removal range if left unchecked.
Turn-one mana dork accelerates the entire gameplan by a full turn.

meta_decks.description: Gruul Landfall ramps with mana dorks into landfall payoffs that snowball with each land drop, threatening lethal by turn 6. Snakeskin Veil punishes spot removal, making timing and sequencing critical for opponents. Meta share: 1.2%.

meta_decks.name: Selesnya Rabbits

key_threats.reason: Lord effect — buffs all rabbits, must remove early
3 tokens at instant speed, enables convoke
Convoke threat that scales, hard to race
Efficient 2-drop, card advantage on attack

meta_decks.description: Go-wide token aggro with rabbit synergies and convoke finishers. #1 deck in meta (~20%). Board wipes and early blockers are key.

meta_decks.name: Grixis Heist

key_threats.reason: Heists 2 spells, drains 2 per heisted cast — snowballs fast
Flexible heist: removal or card advantage
Card advantage engine, draws off big spells

meta_decks.description: Heist-based midrange/control using opponent's cards. Tier 1 (~10-12%). Kill them before they steal your deck's best cards.

meta_decks.name: Orzhov Pixie

key_threats.reason: Creates demon tokens, reusable with Pixie bounce — main win con
Draws cards + lifelink vampire tokens every turn
Enables ETB bounce loops, key engine piece

meta_decks.description: Enchantment bounce engine. Pixie reuses ETBs for value loops. Remove Unholy Annex and Preacher to stop the engine.

meta_decks.name: Orzhov Bats

key_threats.reason: Grinds value, lifelink makes racing impossible
Hand disruption + evasive clock
Removal on a creature, tempo swing

meta_decks.description: Aggressive lifelink/disruption deck with bat synergies. Hard to race due to lifegain. Must out-tempo them.

meta_decks.name: Mono-Black Midrange

key_threats.reason: Must-remove — drains life on every draw, heals on yours
Card advantage + lifelink tokens every turn
Board wipe on ETB against small creatures

meta_decks.description: Removal-heavy midrange with Sheoldred as finisher. Go under them early or go over them with resilient threats.

meta_decks.name: Mono-White Aggro

key_threats.reason: Exile removal + lifelink + ward 2 on one card
Grows huge with enchantments, first strike
Enchantment synergy value engine
Drains X life on attack where X = small creatures; snowballs fast

meta_decks.description: Enchantment-based aggro with strong removal. Watch for Sheltered by Ghosts on their best creature.

meta_decks.name: Boros Tokens

key_threats.reason: Pings on every token creation — free damage adds up fast
2 tokens + anthem = sudden 6+ damage swing
Anthem + protection, hard to wipe board

meta_decks.description: Go-wide token strategy with burn reach. Warleader's Call turns every token into a ping. Don't let them build a board.

meta_decks.name: Rakdos Goblins

key_threats.reason: Doubles goblin count every turn — must remove immediately
Drains on goblin death, makes trades punishing
Grows from creature deaths, menace evasion

meta_decks.description: Goblin tribal with sacrifice/drain synergies. Kill Krenko on sight. They profit from trades via death triggers.

meta_decks.name: Dimir Flash

key_threats.reason: Grows on every flash spell — snowballs quickly
Value engine, creates tokens, hard to ignore
Flying + threshold deathtouch, dangerous blocker and attacker

meta_decks.description: Flash-based tempo. Plays on your turn to grow Cutthroat. Force them to play on their own turn. Remove Cutthroat before it snowballs.

meta_decks.name: Izzet Spells

key_threats.reason: Engine that snowballs spell velocity
Hard-to-race value engine
Bridges tempo into card advantage

meta_decks.description: Spell-heavy tempo/combo with Chorus synergies and prowess. Includes Izzet Lessons and Izzet Chorus variants.

meta_decks.name: Simic Rhythm

key_threats.reason: Enables explosive lethal turns
Accelerates their combo clock
Huge payoff that ends combat math

meta_decks.description: Explosive combo-aggro deck with massive single-turn damage

meta_decks.name: WU Auras/Voltron

key_threats.reason: Grows with every enchantment, main Voltron target
+1/+1 and first strike per enchantment on creature
Exile removal that also grants lifelink

meta_decks.description: Stacks enchantments on one evasive creature. Kill the creature and everything collapses.

meta_decks.name: White (observed)

key_threats.reason: Converts every life gain into a 1/1 Knight token; creates overwhelming boards fast
Grows with each life gain trigger; becomes lethal quickly if unanswered
Anthem effect pumps the wide token board into lethal combat damage
Key enabler that triggers all lifegain payoffs; removing it slows the engine

meta_decks.description: Mono-white lifegain synergy deck that chains cheap life gain triggers into explosive board presence through token generation and +1/+1 counter growth. Dangerous because it converts innocuous life gain into lethal board states that snowball out of control by turn 5-6.
